title: After the Iron Falls
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, exhausted defenders in gate yard, rain easing, broken shields, cinematic, no text
description:
The yard is mud, blood, and splintered courage.

The enemy recoils — not defeated, only delayed.

A messenger finds you: "Councilor Vayne wants you. Now."

choices.label: Go to the council hall for a covert assignment.
If you have 3 key fragments, bind them into a Gatewarden’s Seal.

title: Covert Assignment
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, councilor leaning over map with candles, urgent whispers, cinematic, no text
description:
Vayne speaks without ceremony.

"They tested the gate — and they tested our inside.

There is another way in: old sewers that lead into limestone caves. From there, a forgotten under-fortress sits like a thorn.

If they hold it, they can pour into the city behind the gate. We need a knife in the dark."

choices.label: Accept the mission: descend into the sewers and find the under-fortress approach.
If the gate breached, offer to lead citizens to a safe route first.

title: Inner Streets: Choke Point
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, narrow medieval street barricade, citizens fleeing, defenders forming line, cinematic, no text
description:
A narrow street becomes a funnel. Barrels roll. Planks slam down.

If the gate spill widens, this is where the city dies — or holds.

choices.label: Hold for a short time to let civilians move.
Slip into the sewer access route (covert).

title: Old Sewer Access
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, hidden sewer hatch with lantern, rainwater running into darkness, cinematic, no text
description:
A hatch hidden behind a collapsed brick screen.

Warm air breathes up from below — carrying rot, old water, and something like wet fur.

This route predates the city’s current walls. That’s why it’s dangerous. That’s why it matters.

choices.label: Descend.

title: Sewer Run: Whispering Water
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, sewer tunnel with fast shallow water, echoes, lantern glow, cinematic, no text
description:
Water whispers along the floor, carrying every sound.

You see chalk marks: old smugglers. New gouges: something clawed.

choices.label: Cast Keen Eye to read the marks and find the safer route.
Continue carefully.

title: Split: Brickwork and Stone
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, sewer split with one brick tunnel and one rough stone tunnel, cinematic, no text
description:
Two paths.

Left: newer brick, tighter, with iron brackets.
Right: older stone, rough and damp, opening downward.

The older route smells like caves.

choices.label: Take the brick route (risk of patrols).
Take the old stone route (risk of beasts).

title: Brick Route: Patrol Shadows
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, brick sewer with lantern light ahead, shadowy figures, tense stealth, cinematic, no text
description:
Lantern light ahead.

Voices — not guards. Too harsh. Too amused.

Smugglers… or something worse wearing their confidence.

choices.label: Use Muffle Steps and slip past.
Confront them.

title: Stone Drop
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, rough stone tunnel descending, water dripping, claw marks, cinematic, no text
description:
The stone route slopes down.

Claw marks rake the wall. Something uses this as a corridor — not a home.

Homes are safer than corridors.

choices.label: Proceed (and be ready for vermin).

title: Cave Mouth: Limestone Breath
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, limestone cave mouth beyond sewer arch, mist, faint crystals, cinematic, no text
description:
The sewer ends in a limestone throat.

Mist curls from fissures. In the distance, faint crystals catch your lantern like eyes.

Somewhere deeper, stone echoes with bootsteps — disciplined, not animal.

The under-fortress is real.

choices.label: Enter the caves toward the under-fortress.

title: Cave Junction: The Forked Dark
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, cave junction with two tunnels, one with faint torch soot, one with crystal glimmer, cinematic, no text
description:
Two tunnels.

Left: soot on stone — torches. People.
Right: crystal glimmer — natural… or bait.

If you want the fortress, you follow people. If you want answers, you follow the earth.

choices.label: Follow the soot trail (toward the under-fortress).
Follow the crystal glimmer (side loot / ward shards).

title: Crystal Grotto
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, crystal grotto with glowing shards, shallow pool, eerie beauty, cinematic, no text
description:
Crystals stud the walls like frozen lightning.

In the pool, something metallic gleams — rune-metal, broken into familiar shapes.

Ward shards. Enough to make another stone… or strengthen a seal.

choices.label: Take the ward shards.

title: Under-Fortress Outer Wall
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, ancient underground fortress wall carved into rock, torchlight, skull motifs, cinematic, no text
description:
Stonework rises from rock as if it grew there.

An under-fortress — old as the first wars.

You hear orders in guttural tongues.

If this place feeds the invasion, cutting it will save the city more than any heroics at the gate.

choices.label: Approach the under-fortress entrance.

title: Under-Fortress Entry
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, underground fortress gate with iron bars, torch sconces, guards in silhouette, cinematic, no text
description:
A barred entry with two sentries.

Beyond the bars: a corridor, disciplined footsteps, and the smell of oil.

This is where your city questing becomes war.

choices.label: Proceed into Under-Fortress Scenario I (next chapter).
